title: livespec_config
description: Schema for the project's `.livespec.jsonc` configuration file. All fields are optional; absence of the file or any field is equivalent to its documented default. The root is `additionalProperties: true` per contracts.md §"Plugin distribution": each plugin or sibling consumer owns a top-level section named for itself; core validates only the keys it owns and tolerates unknown sections.

template.description: Active template — built-in name (e.g. 'livespec', 'livespec-with-diagrams', 'minimal') or path-as-string to a custom template directory relative to the project root.

spec_root.description: Project-root-relative directory holding the governed spec tree. Spec-tier fact per contracts.md §"Spec-side CLI contract".

template_format_version.description: Expected template format version. Doctor's `template-version-matches` check validates that the template's own `template_format_version` equals this value.

post_step_skip_doctor_llm_objective_chec.description: When true, post-step doctor (run automatically after seed/propose-change/critique/revise) skips the LLM-driven objective-checks phase.

post_step_skip_doctor_llm_subjective_che.description: When true, post-step doctor skips the LLM-driven subjective-checks phase.

pre_step_skip_static_checks.description: When true, sub-commands skip the pre-step static check. Intended for emergency recovery only; the skill MUST narrate a warning every time pre-step is skipped.

post_step_skip_capture_impl_gaps.description: When true, the revise sub-command skips the end-of-revise `capture-impl-gaps` post-step invocation against the active impl plugin. The revise SKILL.md prose MUST narrate a warning every time the post-step is silently skipped (config key true with no CLI flag).

pre_step_skip_stale_branch_check.description: When true, the revise sub-command skips the Step 3.5 stale-branch precondition check (which enumerates local `spec/*` branches ahead of `origin/<canonical_branch>` via the `no_stale_revise_branches` shared check shipped by livespec-dev-tooling). The revise SKILL.md prose MUST narrate a warning every time the precondition is silently skipped (config key true with no CLI flag).

spec_clis.description: Per contracts.md §"Spec-side CLI contract": each spec-side lifecycle operation is named here as an argv-form array (NOT a shell string), pre-populated with core's reference default and individually overridable. The literal substring `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` in any argv entry is expanded at dispatch time to the installed livespec plugin root (the `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` env var when set; the running package's plugin root otherwise). Callability of every named entry is verified by the `config-named-cli-callability` doctor invariant per contracts.md §"Doctor cross-boundary invariants". When the section (or any key) is absent the validator materializes the documented per-key defaults.

propose_change.description: argv-form CLI for the `propose-change` operation (the one spec-side CLI injected into the orchestrator's drift-capture CLI).

doctor.description: argv-form CLI for the `doctor` operation. NOT privileged: config-named and overridable like any other spec-side CLI.

orchestrator.description: Per contracts.md §"Orchestrator CLI contract — the three named CLIs": selects the orchestrator that consumes the spec and produces the implementation, and names its three orchestrator-side CLIs as argv-form arrays. LiveSpec defines NONE of their behavior — only that they are named here and callable (verified by the `config-named-cli-callability` doctor invariant). OPTIONAL: when the section is absent, no orchestrator is configured and only the spec-side CLIs are verified. Orchestrator-private configuration (store formats, store paths, …) lives in the orchestrator's own top-level section, which core's schema does not know.

name.description: The orchestrator's name (e.g. 'livespec-orchestrator-beads-fabro'). By convention the orchestrator owns the top-level config section bearing this name.

spec_reader.description: argv-form spec-reader CLI. Reads the spec however it wants; MUST expose spec content by template category (categorizes, never conceals).

gap_capture.description: argv-form gap-capture CLI. Detects spec → impl gaps and writes them to the orchestrator's private work-item mechanism; the spec-reader CLI is injected as a reference.

drift_capture.description: argv-form drift-capture CLI. Routes impl → spec drift to propose-change; the spec-reader CLI and the propose-change CLI are injected as references.

credential_wrapper.description: OPTIONAL argv-form command prefix that injects the project's credentials/secrets into the process environment before a downstream CLI runs (e.g. the reference 1Password Environment wrapper `["/usr/local/bin/with-livespec-env.sh", "--"]`). Backend-agnostic: livespec constrains only that it is a non-empty array of strings when present; the secret backend, the wrapper identity, and the injected variables are the project's own concern. The literal substring `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` in any entry is expanded at dispatch time as elsewhere. When the key is absent the validator materializes `[]`, meaning no wrapper is applied.
